title: [circt-bmc] Functionality to turn off assertion failure for specific clock period

features.name: sequential register with clock and no reset
features.description: Code should include a sequential register (seq.compreg) clocked on a clock signal without an explicit reset, where the register output feeds into assertion logic

features.name: assertion comparing combinational logic outputs across two circuit variants
features.description: Code should include a verification assertion comparing equality of two combinational logic paths that produce results from registers with different pipeline depths
features.snippet:
verif.assert %cmp_result : i1
  // where cmp_result = comb.icmp eq %path_a, %path_b

features.name: register pipeline stage mismatch
features.description: Code should include two registers with different pipeline depths where the shallower register feeds into an assertion before both have stabilized

features.name: module instantiation with clock and reset
features.description: Code should include hardware module instantiation with clock, reset, and multiple data inputs passed as arguments
features.snippet: hw.instance "mod" @ModuleName(clock: %clk: !seq.clock, reset: %rst: i1, a: %data: i1) -> (out: i1)

features.name: chained combinational AND gates
features.description: Code should include multiple chained comb.and operations feeding into a register or assertion
